parse:
run {
  #User = 2
  #Photo = 2
  distinct u1, u2 : User
  distinct p1, p2 : Photo
  u1.posts = p1
  u2.posts = p2
  // Ensure all photos are covered
  all p : Photo | p in u1.posts or p in u2.posts
  everyPhotoPostedByOneUser
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  #User = 3
  #Photo = 3
  distinct u1, u2, u3 : User
  distinct p1, p2, p3 : Photo
  u1.posts = p1
  u2.posts = p2
  u3.posts = p3
  // Ensure all photos are covered
  all p : Photo | p in u1.posts or p in u2.posts or p in u3.posts
  everyPhotoPostedByOneUser
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  #Photo = 2
  #User = 1
  distinct p1, p2 : Photo
  one u1 : User
  u1.posts = p1 // p1 is posted by u1
  no u : User | p2 in u.posts // p2 is unposted
  everyPhotoPostedByOneUser
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  #Photo = 2
  #User = 2
  distinct p1, p2 : Photo
  distinct u1, u2 : User
  u1.posts = p1 // p1 is posted by u1 only
  p2 in u1.posts and p2 in u2.posts // p2 is posted by u1 and u2
  everyPhotoPostedByOneUser
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one u1: User
  one p1: Ad
  one d1: Day

  u1.posts = p1
  u1.sees = p1
  p1.date = d1
  no u1.follows

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one u1, u2: User
  u1 != u2
  one p1: Photo - Ad
  one d1: Day

  u1.follows = u2
  u2.posts = p1
  u1.sees = p1
  p1.date = d1
  no Ad

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one u1, u2, u3: User
  u1 != u2 and u1 != u3 and u2 != u3
  one p1: Photo - Ad
  one p2: Ad
  one d1, d2: Day
  d1 != d2

  u1.follows = u2
  u2.posts = p1
  u3.posts = p2
  p1.date = d1
  p2.date = d2

  u1.sees = p1 + p2
  u2.sees = p2
  u3.sees = p2

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one u1: User
  one p1: Photo - Ad
  one d1: Day

  u1.posts = p1
  u1.follows = u1
  u1.sees = p1
  p1.date = d1
  no Ad

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one u1, u2: User
  u1 != u2
  one p1: Ad
  one d1: Day

  u2.posts = p1
  u1.sees = none
  p1.date = d1

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one u1, u2: User
  u1 != u2
  one p1: Photo - Ad
  one d1: Day

  u2.posts = p1
  u1.sees = p1
  no u1.follows
  p1.date = d1
  no Ad

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one u1: User
  one p1: Photo - Ad
  one d1: Day

  u1.posts = p1
  u1.sees = p1
  no u1.follows
  p1.date = d1
  no Ad

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one u1, u2, u3, u4: User
  all disj x, y: {u1, u2, u3, u4} | x != y

  one p1: Ad
  one p2: Photo - Ad
  one d1, d2: Day
  d1 != d2

  u2.posts = p1
  u4.posts = p2
  p1.date = d1
  p2.date = d2

  u1.sees = none
  u3.sees = p2
  no u3.follows

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one u1: User
  one p1: Photo - Ad
  one d1: Day

  u1.sees = p1
  no User.posts
  p1.date = d1
  no Ad

  (all u: User, p: Photo | p in Ad and (some poster: User | p in poster.posts) implies p in u.sees) and
  (all u: User, p: Photo | p not in Ad and p in u.sees implies (some f: u.follows | p in f.posts))
} for 4 expect 0

run InfluencersPostEveryDay {
  one i : Influencer
  some d1, d2, d3 : Day | d1 != d2 and d2 != d3 and d1 != d3
  some p1 : Photo | p1.date = d1 and p1 in i.posts
  some p2 : Photo | p2.date = d2 and p2 in i.posts
  no p_on_d3_by_i : i.posts | p_on_d3_by_i.date = d3 // 'i' missed 'd3'
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  (some u1, u_followed, u_suggested : User, a_followed, a_suggested : Ad | (
    (u1 != u_followed and u1 != u_suggested and u_followed != u_suggested) and // Ensure u1, u_followed, u_suggested are distinct
    (a_followed != a_suggested) and // Ensure ads are distinct

    (a_followed in u_followed.posts) and
    (u_followed in u1.follows) and
    (a_followed in u1.sees) and

    (a_suggested in u_suggested.posts) and
    (u_suggested in u1.suggested) and
    (a_suggested in u1.sees)
  )) and userOnlySeesAdsFromFollowedOrSuggested
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  (some u1, u_ad_poster, u_photo_poster : User, a1 : Ad, p1 : Photo - Ad | (
    distinct u1, u_ad_poster, u_photo_poster and

    a1 in u_ad_poster.posts and
    u_ad_poster in u1.follows and // Ad poster is followed
    a1 in u1.sees and

    p1 in u_photo_poster.posts and
    not (u_photo_poster in u1.follows or u_photo_poster in u1.suggested) and // Non-ad poster is neither followed nor suggested
    p1 in u1.sees // User u1 sees this non-ad photo
  )) and userOnlySeesAdsFromFollowedOrSuggested
} for 4 expect 1
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and some Human and no Robot } for 4 expect 1
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and some Robot and no Human } for 4 expect 1
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and some Human and some Robot } for 4 expect 1
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and no Worker } for 4 expect 1
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and #Human = 2 and #Robot = 1 and #Worker = 3 } for 4 expect 1
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and (some Worker and no Human and no Robot) } for 4 expect 0
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and (some (Worker - (Human + Robot)) and some Human and no Robot) } for 4 expect 0
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and (some (Worker - (Human + Robot)) and some Robot and no Human) } for 4 expect 0
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and (some (Worker - (Human + Robot)) and some Human and some Robot) } for 4 expect 0
run { NoWorkerIsNeitherHumanNorRobot and (#(Worker - (Human + Robot)) = 2 and #Human = 1 and #Robot = 1 and #Worker = 4) } for 4 expect 0

run N4_WorkstationEmptyAndWorkerUnassigned {
  #Workstation = 2
  #Worker = 2
  (some ws: Workstation | no ws.workers) // Violation 1: An empty workstation
  and (some wk: Worker | no wk.~workers) // Violation 2: An unassigned worker
  req_WorkstationWorkerAssignment
} for 4 expect 0

run N5_WorkstationEmptyAndWorkerInMultipleWorkstations {
  #Workstation = 3 // Need enough for an empty workstation and for one worker to be in two
  #Worker = 1
  (some ws: Workstation | no ws.workers) // Violation 1: An empty workstation
  and (some wk: Worker | #wk.~workers > 1) // Violation 2: A worker in multiple workstations
  req_WorkstationWorkerAssignment
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some w_violating, w_clean : Workstation | w_violating != w_clean
  some h : Human, r : Robot
  h in w_violating.workers
  r in w_violating.workers
  humansAndRobotsCannotWorkTogether
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one c1, c2: Component
  one p1, p2: Product
  c1 != c2
  p1 != c1 && p1 != c2
  p2 != c1 && p2 != c2
  c1.parts = p1
  c2.parts = p2
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c1, c2: Component
  one m1, m2: Material
  c1 != c2
  m1 != m2
  c1.parts = m1
  c2.parts = c1 // c2 has c1 as a part. Since c1 != c2, c2 is not its own part.
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c1, c2: Component
  one m1: Material
  c1 != c2
  c1.parts = m1 + c1 // c1 is explicitly its own part
  c2.parts = m1 // c2 is not its own part
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some dcomp : (Dangerous & Component)
  some h : Human
  some r : Robot
  some w_dangerous_robot_only : Workstation

  w_dangerous_robot_only in dcomp.workstation
  r in w_dangerous_robot_only.workers
  no (Human & w_dangerous_robot_only.workers) // This workstation has NO humans

  // Ensure that for ANY workstation processing ANY dangerous component, no human works there.
  all ws : Workstation | (some dc : (Dangerous & Component) | ws in dc.workstation) implies (no (ws.workers & Human))
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  some dcomp : (Dangerous & Component)
  some h : Human
  some r : Robot
  some w_danger_robot_only : Workstation
  some w_human_only : Workstation
  
  w_danger_robot_only in dcomp.workstation
  r in w_danger_robot_only.workers
  no (Human & w_danger_robot_only.workers)
  
  h in w_human_only.workers
  no (dcomp.workstation & w_human_only)
  
  // The key condition for P5: all workstations with humans must not process dangerous components.
  all w_h : Workstation | (some h_w : Human | h_w in w_h.workers) implies (no ((Dangerous & Component).workstation & w_h))
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  some dcomp : (Dangerous & Component)
  some h : Human
  some w : Workstation
  w in dcomp.workstation
  h in w.workers
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some dcomp : (Dangerous & Component)
  some h : Human
  some w : Workstation
  w in dcomp.workstation
  w.workers = h // The workstation only has this human worker
  dcomp.workstation = w // The component is only processed at this workstation
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some dcomp1, dcomp2 : (Dangerous & Component)
  some h1, h2 : Human
  some r1 : Robot
  some w1, w2 : Workstation
  
  // Violation: dcomp1 is processed at w1, and h1 works at w1
  w1 in dcomp1.workstation
  h1 in w1.workers
  
  // Other setup (not violating by itself)
  w2 in dcomp2.workstation
  r1 in w2.workers
  no (Human & w2.workers) // w2 processes dcomp2 safely with a robot
  h2 in w1.workers // Another human works at the violating workstation
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some dcomp : (Dangerous & Component)
  some h : Human
  some r : Robot
  some w : Workstation
  w in dcomp.workstation // dcomp processed at w
  h in w.workers         // h works at w (violates the rule)
  r in w.workers         // r also works at w (shows it's a mixed workforce)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some dcomp : (Dangerous & Component)
  some h : Human
  some r : Robot
  some w_human_assem : Workstation
  some w_robot_assem : Workstation
  
  w_human_assem in dcomp.workstation
  h in w_human_assem.workers // The workstation handling dcomp has a human (violation)
  
  w_robot_assem in dcomp.workstation
  r in w_robot_assem.workers // The other workstation handling dcomp has a robot
  
  w_human_assem != w_robot_assem // Ensure they are distinct workstations
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  // C1 has two component parts, P1 and P2, processed at different earlier workstations
  some w1 : Workstation - begin - end {
    begin.succ = end // P1 (at begin) before C1 (at end)
    w1.succ = end     // P2 (at w1) before C1 (at end)
    no end.succ
    no (Workstation - begin - end - w1).succ
  }
  some c1 : Component, disj p1, p2 : Component { // p1, p2 are distinct component parts
    c1.parts = p1 + p2
    p1.workstation = begin
    p2.workstation = w1
    c1.workstation = end
    no c1.parts - p1 - p2
    no c1.workstation - end
    no p1.workstation - begin
    no p2.workstation - w1
  }
  no Workstation.succ - (begin -> end) - (w1 -> end)
  no Worker
  no Material
  no Dangerous
  no Component - c1 - p1 - p2
  PartsAssembledBeforeComponent
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  // Component C1 has two component parts. One (P1) is before C1, but the other (P2) is after C1.
  some w1 : Workstation - begin - end {
    begin.succ = w1 // Path from begin to w1
    w1.succ = end   // Path from w1 to end
    no end.succ
    no (Workstation - begin - end - w1).succ
  }
  some c1 : Component, disj p1, p2 : Component {
    c1.parts = p1 + p2
    p1.workstation = begin // P1 at begin (before C1)
    p2.workstation = end   // P2 at end (after C1)
    c1.workstation = w1    // C1 at w1
    no c1.parts - p1 - p2
    no c1.workstation - w1
    no p1.workstation - begin
    no p2.workstation - end
  }
  no Workstation.succ - (begin -> w1) - (w1 -> end)
  no Worker
  no Material
  no Dangerous
  no Component - c1 - p1 - p2
  PartsAssembledBeforeComponent
} for 4 expect 0

consistent:
run {
  some disj t1, t2, t3, t4 : Track {
    Track = t1 + t2 + t3 + t4
    
    Exit = t1 // t1 is an Exit track
    t1.succs = t2 // VIOLATION: t1 is in Exit but has a successor
    
    // Ensure other tracks conform to isolate the violation
    no (t2 + t3 + t4) & Exit // t2, t3, t4 are not Exit
    t2.succs = t3
    t3.succs = t4
    t4.succs = t1
    
    no Junction
    no Entry
    some Semaphore
    some Signal
  }
  all t: Track | t in Exit iff no t.succs
} expect 0

run {
  some disj t1, t2, t3, t4 : Track {
    Track = t1 + t2 + t3 + t4
    
    no (Exit & t1) // t1 is explicitly NOT an Exit track
    no t1.succs // VIOLATION: t1 has no successors but is not an Exit track
    
    // Set up other tracks to be valid
    Exit = t2 // t2 is an Exit track
    no t2.succs // t2 conforms: is Exit and has no successors
    
    no (t3 + t4) & Exit // t3, t4 are not Exit
    t3.succs = t2 // t3 conforms: not Exit and has successors
    t4.succs = t3 // t4 conforms: not Exit and has successors
    
    no Junction
    no Entry
    some Semaphore
    some Signal
  }
  all t: Track | t in Exit iff no t.succs
} expect 0

run {
  some disj t1, t2, t3, t4 : Track {
    Track = t1 + t2 + t3 + t4
    
    Exit = t1 + t2 // t1 and t2 are Exit tracks
    no t1.succs // t1 conforms: is Exit and has no successors
    t2.succs = t3 // VIOLATION: t2 is an Exit track but has a successor
    
    // Ensure t3, t4 are not Exit and conform
    no (t3 + t4) & Exit
    t3.succs = t4
    t4.succs = t1
    
    no Junction
    no Entry
    some Semaphore
    some Signal
  }
  all t: Track | t in Exit iff no t.succs
} expect 0

run {
  some disj t1, t2, t3, t4 : Track {
    Track = t1 + t2 + t3 + t4
    
    Exit = t1 // Only t1 is Exit and has no successors (compliant)
    no t1.succs
    
    // t2, t3, t4 are not Exit
    no (t2 + t3 + t4) & Exit
    t2.succs = t3
    no t4.succs // VIOLATION: t4 is not Exit but has no successors
    
    t3.succs = t1
    
    no Junction
    no Entry
    some Semaphore
    some Signal
  }
  all t: Track | t in Exit iff no t.succs
} expect 0

run {
  some disj t1, t2, t3, t4 : Track {
    Track = t1 + t2 + t3 + t4
    
    Exit = t1 // t1 is declared Exit
    t1.succs = t2 // Violation 1: t1 is Exit, but has a successor
    
    no (t2 + t3 + t4) & Exit // t2, t3, t4 are NOT Exit
    no t3.succs // Violation 2: t3 is NOT Exit, but has NO successor
    
    // Ensure other tracks are consistent
    t2.succs = t4
    t4.succs = t1
    
    no Junction
    no Entry
    some Semaphore
    some Signal
  }
  all t: Track | t in Exit iff no t.succs
} expect 0

parse:
run {
  one e: Entry, one ex: Exit
  e.succs = {ex} // Explicitly assign a set
  no Junction // Simplify the graph structure
  no (Entry - e) // Ensure only one Entry atom
  no (Exit - ex) // Ensure only one Exit atom
  no (Track - (e + ex)) // Ensure only these two Track atoms exist
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs // The requirement
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one e: Entry, one j: Junction, one ex: Exit
  e.succs = {j} // Explicitly assign a set
  j.succs = {ex} // Explicitly assign a set
  no (Entry - e)
  no (Junction - j)
  no (Exit - ex)
  no (Track - (e + j + ex))
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one e: Entry
  some ex1, ex2: Exit | ex1 != ex2 // Two distinct Exit atoms
  e.succs = ex1 + ex2 // Already a set, no change needed
  no Junction
  no (Entry - e)
  no (Exit - (ex1 + ex2))
  no (Track - (e + ex1 + ex2))
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  some e1, e2: Entry | e1 != e2 // Two distinct Entry atoms
  one ex: Exit
  e1.succs = {ex} // Explicitly assign a set
  e2.succs = {ex} // Explicitly assign a set
  no Junction
  no (Entry - (e1 + e2))
  no (Exit - ex)
  no (Track - (e1 + e2 + ex))
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  some e1, e2: Entry | e1 != e2
  some ex1, ex2: Exit | ex1 != ex2
  e1.succs = ex1 + ex2 // Already a set, no change needed
  e2.succs = ex1 + ex2 // Already a set, no change needed
  no Junction
  no (Entry - (e1 + e2))
  no (Exit - (ex1 + ex2))
  // Allow other Track atoms for scope 4, as long as they don't break connectivity
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one e: Entry, one ex: Exit
  no succs // No 'succs' relations exist anywhere in the model
  no Junction
  no (Entry - e)
  no (Exit - ex)
  no (Track - (e + ex))
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs // The requirement (expected to be false)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some e1, e2: Entry | e1 != e2
  one ex: Exit
  e1.succs = {ex} // Explicitly assign a set
  no e2.succs // e2 is isolated, cannot reach ex
  no Junction
  no (Entry - (e1 + e2))
  no (Exit - ex)
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one e: Entry
  some ex1, ex2: Exit | ex1 != ex2
  e.succs = {ex1} // Explicitly assign a set
  no (succs.ex2) // No track leads to ex2, making it unreachable
  no Junction
  no (Entry - e)
  no (Exit - (ex1 + ex2))
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one e: Entry, one ex: Exit
  one t: Track - (e + ex + Junction) // An intermediate track not directly connected to exit
  e.succs = {t} // Explicitly assign a set. Entry leads to this intermediate track
  t.succs = {t} // Explicitly assign a set. The intermediate track forms a self-loop, a dead end
  no (e.*succs & ex) // Explicitly ensure ex is not in the transitive closure of e, enforcing no path
                     // This condition combined with the requirement makes the run unsatisfiable.
  no Junction
  no (Entry - e)
  no (Exit - ex)
  all e': Entry, ex': Exit | ex' in e'.*succs
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one c1, c2: Course
  c1 != c2
  one p1, p2: Person
  p1 != p2
  p1.teaches = c1
  p2.teaches = c2
  CoursesMustHaveTeachers
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c1, c2: Course
  c1 != c2
  one p: Person
  p.teaches = c1 + c2
  CoursesMustHaveTeachers
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c: Course
  one p1, p2: Person
  p1 != p2
  p1.teaches = c
  p2.teaches = c
  CoursesMustHaveTeachers
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  some c1, c2, c3: Course
  distinct c1, c2, c3
  some p1, p2: Person
  distinct p1, p2
  Course = c1 + c2 + c3
  Person = p1 + p2
  p1.teaches = c1 + c2
  p2.teaches = c2 + c3
  CoursesMustHaveTeachers
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c_untaught, c_taught: Course
  distinct c_untaught, c_taught
  one p: Person
  p.teaches = c_taught
  all p_any: Person | c_untaught not in p_any.teaches
  CoursesMustHaveTeachers
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one untaught_c, taught_c: Course
  distinct untaught_c, taught_c
  one prof: Professor
  one student: Student
  prof.teaches = taught_c
  no student.teaches
  all p: Person | untaught_c not in p.teaches
  CoursesMustHaveTeachers
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one untaught_c, c1, c2: Course
  distinct untaught_c, c1, c2
  one p1, p2: Person
  distinct p1, p2
  Course = untaught_c + c1 + c2
  Person = p1 + p2
  p1.teaches = c1
  p2.teaches = c2
  // untaught_c is not in p1.teaches and not in p2.teaches, so it's not taught by anyone.
  CoursesMustHaveTeachers
} for 4 expect 0

valid:
run {
    some p1, p2, p3: Professor, c1, c2: Course, s1: Student |
        p1 != p2 and
        c1 in p1.teaches and
        c1 in p2.enrolled and // Violation 1: p1 teaches p2
        p3 != p1 and p3 != p2 and
        c2 in p3.teaches and
        c2 in s1.enrolled // Valid (p3 teaches s1), but the first violation holds
} for 4 expect 0

run {
    some p1, p2: Professor, c1, c2: Course |
        p1 != p2 and
        c1 in p1.teaches and
        c1 in p2.enrolled and // Violation 1: p1 teaches p2
        c2 in p2.teaches and
        c2 in p1.enrolled  // Violation 2: p2 teaches p1 (mutual teaching)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
    some p1, p2, p3: Professor, c1, c2: Course |
        p1 != p2 and p1 != p3 and p2 != p3 and
        c1 in p1.teaches and
        c1 in p2.enrolled and // Violation 1: p1 teaches p2 in c1
        c2 in p1.teaches and
        c2 in p3.enrolled  // Violation 2: p1 teaches p3 in c2
} for 4 expect 0

parse:
run {
  all s: Student, c: Course | lone (c.grades[s]) // The requirement holds
  exactly 1 Student as s_inst
  exactly 1 Course as c_inst
  exactly 1 Grade as g_inst
  c_inst.grades[s_inst] = g_inst
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  all s: Student, c: Course | lone (c.grades[s]) // The requirement holds
  exactly 1 Student as s_inst
  exactly 1 Course as c_inst
  some Grade // Grades may exist, but not assigned for this student/course
  no c_inst.grades[s_inst]
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  all s: Student, c: Course | lone (c.grades[s]) // The requirement holds
  some disj s1, s2: Student
  some disj c1, c2: Course
  some disj g1, g2: Grade
  c1.grades[s1] = g1
  c2.grades[s2] = g2
  no c1.grades[s2] // s2 has no grade for c1
  no c2.grades[s1] // s1 has no grade for c2
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  some s_viol: Student, c_viol: Course | some disj g_a, g_b: (c_viol.grades[s_viol]) // The negation of the requirement holds
  
  exactly 1 Student as s_inst
  some disj c1, c2: Course // Two distinct courses
  some disj g1, g2: Grade // Two distinct grades for the violation
  some g3: Grade // A third grade for the consistent course (optional but makes instance richer)

  c1.grades[s_inst] = g1 + g2 // Violation for s_inst in c1
  lone (c2.grades[s_inst]) // c2 might be consistent (e.g., c2.grades[s_inst] = g3 or no grade)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some s_viol: Student, c_viol: Course | some disj g_a, g_b: (c_viol.grades[s_viol]) // The negation of the requirement holds
  
  some disj s1, s2: Student
  exactly 1 Course as c_inst
  some disj g1, g2: Grade // Two distinct grades for the violation
  some g3: Grade // A third grade for the consistent student (optional)

  c_inst.grades[s1] = g1 + g2 // Violation for s1 in c_inst
  lone (c_inst.grades[s2]) // s2 might be consistent (e.g., c_inst.grades[s2] = g3 or no grade)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some s_viol: Student, c_viol: Course | some disj g_a, g_b: (c_viol.grades[s_viol]) // The negation of the requirement holds
  
  exactly 1 Professor as p_inst
  exactly 1 Project as proj_inst
  exactly 2 Grade as g1, g2 // Ensure distinct grades for violation
  exactly 1 Student as s_inst // focus on one student for the violation
  exactly 1 Course as c_inst // focus on one course for the violation
  
  // The direct violation
  c_inst.grades[s_inst] = g1 + g2
  
  // Add some relations to enrich the instance, ensuring they don't prevent the violation
  p_inst.teaches = c_inst // Professor teaches the course where violation occurs
  s_inst.enrolled = c_inst // This student is enrolled in the course with violation
  proj_inst in c_inst.projects // This course has one project
  
} for 4 expect 0

run PositiveInstance2_SameGrade {
  some c: Course, p: Project, s1, s2: Student, g: Grade |
    s1 != s2 and
    s1 in c.enrolled and s2 in c.enrolled and
    p in c.projects and p in s1.projects and p in s2.projects and
    c.grades[s1] = g and c.grades[s2] = g
  NoLargeGradeDifference
} for 4 expect 1

run PositiveInstance3_OneUnitDiff {
  some c: Course, p: Project, s1, s2: Student, g_lower: Grade |
    s1 != s2 and
    s1 in c.enrolled and s2 in c.enrolled and
    p in c.projects and p in s1.projects and p in s2.projects and
    some g_higher: Grade | g_higher = g_lower.next and
    c.grades[s1] = g_lower and c.grades[s2] = g_higher
  NoLargeGradeDifference
} for 4 expect 1

run PositiveInstance4_ComplexCompliant {
  some s1, s2, s3: Student, c1, c2: Course, p1, p2: Project, gA, gB: Grade |
    s1 != s2 and s1 != s3 and s2 != s3 and // three distinct students
    c1 != c2 and p1 != p2 and // two distinct courses and projects
    gB = gA.next and // two grades, one next to the other (e.g., G1, G2)

    // s1, s2 on p1 in c1, with grades gA, gB (compliant)
    s1 in c1.enrolled and s2 in c1.enrolled and
    p1 in c1.projects and p1 in s1.projects and p1 in s2.projects and
    c1.grades[s1] = gA and c1.grades[s2] = gB and

    // s3 on p2 in c2, with grade gA (no other distinct student on p2 in c2, so no violation possible for this specific project)
    s3 in c2.enrolled and p2 in c2.projects and p2 in s3.projects and
    c2.grades[s3] = gA
  NoLargeGradeDifference
} for 4 expect 1
